$comment: Reproducer-as-gate-verdict manifest (#407). For PRNG-free non-heavy gate rows the standard pitch-trackers cannot grade (polyphonic/method-asymmetry/onset-blind), we grade an instrument-friendly PROJECTION that exercises the SAME engine logic on gradeable material, and record ITS verdict as the gate verdict — annotated 'graded via projection'. The raw sweep (examples-sweep.json) keeps the unvarnished INCONCL verdicts; this overlay is the launch-gate computation. tools/gate-report.ts consumes this.
denominator: PRNG-free non-heavy official rows (per launch_acceptance_criteria / dharana §28)

reproducers.blindReason: #374 — polyphonic play_chord arpeggios; contour tracker emits out-of-set noise on BOTH sides (desktop pc-set {0,4,5,6,7,8,9,10,11} vs web {0,1,4,5,8,9,10,11}); no affirmative MATCH extractable from the real capture.
reproducers.projection: Arpeggiate the same chord_degree(d,:c,:major,3,invert:i) chords + staccato (release 0.05, sleep 0.5) → onset conf 1. Grades the exact engine logic under test (chord_degree + inversion), synth/voicing-independent.

evidence.note: PITCH-MATCH over 15 notes; re-captured live on merged main (#405+#409)

reproducers.blindReason: #376 — method asymmetry: the heavy_kick dominates web's onset tracker while desktop locks onto the sustained mod_saw (desktop onset pc {3,4,5} vs web contour pc {0,2,4,5,7}); non-comparable.
reproducers.projection: Isolate the DETERMINISTIC :synths ring (:F,:C,:D,:D,:G,:C,:D,:D) — note(n, octave: 2) monophonic + staccato, :beep (pitch logic is synth-independent; mod_saw FM confuses the onset tracker ~1 semitone). Grades note-name + octave resolution + ring.
requires: #409 note(octave:) fix (MERGED 8f38e2c) — without it web played +24 semitones; the projection itself surfaced this engine bug, SP106

evidence.note: PITCH-MATCH over 15 notes; re-captured live on merged main; /s_new ground truth confirms ring@oct2 = 41,36,38,38,43 (F2,C2,D2,D2,G2)

limitations.reason: Sustained :beep polyphony — desktop onset detector finds ~3 onsets in 9s (#368); the web capture renders the full ~95s composition vs the windowed desktop capture (#406). No faithful gradeable projection: a monophonic literal-note list would test almost nothing (bach's substance is timing + concurrent in_thread alignment, exactly what's not onset-gradeable).
limitations.engineEvidence: Engine verified correct via the deterministic event-log timeline (#404/#405): web spans 95.2s ≈ the 95.5s score, section-2 RH (note 83) lands at t=48.0/72.0 — exact score anchors. For a no-PRNG piece the event log IS ground truth for scheduling.

limitations.reason: One sustained note (:cs1) + a sample texture, not a melody — Tier-1 pitch comparison is the wrong lens entirely. A reproducer would test a single note, which is not meaningful musical-correctness coverage.
limitations.engineEvidence: No pitch-sequence to verify; the relevant engine paths (FX chain routing, sample playback) are covered by the FX-sweep / sample tooling, not Tier-1 pitch.

limitations.reason: REGRESSION-EXPOSED by SV55 (#419/#420), re-measured 2026-05-30 on merged main: web is now SILENT (peak 0.0000, RMS 0.0000) while desktop plays (peak 0.4564). Pre-SV55 the top-level use_synth :tb303 was dropped, web played :beep, and that accidentally produced a coincidental Tier-1 PITCH-MATCH (both ~36.7Hz, tracker read 45) — the stale 2026-05-29 'raw-refreshed MATCH' was that masked :beep. Post-SV55 web correctly plays :tb303 and the latent FX-bus silent-failure surfaces (#423). Does NOT count as pass — web produces no audio for this official example.
limitations.engineEvidence: Isolation (Lokayata): tb303 plain=audible 0.256 · tb303+nested-FX (no loop)=audible 0.269 · tb303+live_loop+sync (no FX)=audible 0.256 · all three combined (real)=SILENT 0.000. Bug = trailing bare nested with_fx after a concurrent live_loop+sync routes FX output to an unmonitored bus (B2). Filed #423.

exclusions.reason: Uses choose([:D4,:E4]) etc. — PRNG-driven. Cross-engine PRNG parity is a declared v1 NON-GOAL (SV49), so it is excluded from the PRNG-free gate denominator (it belongs with the other ~22 PRNG rows). The post-SP104 framing wrongly counted it in the denom.
